---

title: "Getting Started with RpkgR"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
%\VignetteIndexEntry{Getting Started with RpkgR}
%\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
\usepackage[utf8]{inputenc}
---------------------------

# Introduction

RpkgR provides tools for creating, managing, validating, and deploying local R package repositories in regulated and restricted-network environments.

# Repository Creation

Create a local package repository.

```r
repo_path <- tempdir()

pkgr_create_repo(
  repo_path = repo_path
)
```

# Dependency Resolution

Review package dependencies and repository completeness.

```r
pkgr_dependency_summary(
  package = "Tplyr",
  repo_path = repo_path
)
```

# Required Files

Generate a list of package files required for installation.

```r
pkgr_required_files(
  package = "Tplyr"
)
```

# Repository Validation

Validate repository contents.

```r
pkgr_validate_repository(
  repo_path = repo_path
)
```

# Repository Health

Assess repository completeness and health.

```r
pkgr_repository_health(
  repo_path = repo_path
)
```

# Package Bundles

Create package bundles for controlled deployment.

```r
pkgr_build_bundle(
  package = "Tplyr"
)
```

# Conclusion

RpkgR provides a reproducible framework for package repository management, dependency resolution, validation, deployment, and reporting workflows.
